themes.exampleQuote: The strawberry matcha here might be the best I've ever had.
I just wish this place was bigger, gets packed quickly!
mine was definitely on the sweet side
the workers are so nice every time I visit!
The wi-fi isn't good enough to work from here so I wouldn't come back for that.

notable_quote: The strawberry matcha here might be the best I've ever had — the coffee, food, and overall cafe vibe is fantastic.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently return for the specialty drinks — the strawberry matcha and tiramisu latte pull most of the enthusiasm — while a small number flag the cramped footprint, inconsistent order accuracy, and weak wifi as reasons not to make it a work-from-cafe habit.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-six Pittsburgh creators filed into Delanie's Coffee Shadyside this quarter and posted 30-plus TikToks; Delanie's itself posted three times in the last month and filmed none of it back. The city is doing the storytelling, and the business is mostly a spectator — @tessadecario's tiramisu latte clip alone drew 37,200 plays from a caption that could have lived on Delanie's own feed. Meanwhile, Allegheny Coffee & Tea Exchange holds the Cafes category on the strength of its social momentum, a signal Delanie's has already been handed by its creators but hasn't picked up. The rank moves if the business starts showing up in the same places its customers already are.
tldr_read: 26 creators filming, 3 posts in 30 days, one of the strongest creator-fit readings in the family — Ones to Watch, #4 in Pittsburgh Coffee Shops.
tldr_meaning: The creator coverage is already there; the gap closing between what Pittsburgh is posting about Delanie's and what Delanie's posts about itself is what separates this ranking from the tier above it.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: 26 Creators Are Filming This Place, And The Place Is Filming None Of It Back

playbook.headline: Post what creators are already making
playbook.action: Repost or recreate the tiramisu latte and strawberry matcha content that 26 creators have already validated — the menu and the audience are proven, the account just needs to show up.

playbook.headline: Build the visual catalog past its floor
playbook.action: With 96 photos spread thin across only 6 active Google categories, adding 15-20 dedicated interior and drink photos would bring the visual catalog closer to the family median and give new customers something to land on before visiting.

playbook.headline: Turn the order-error review into a policy signal
playbook.action: The three-star review about a wrong muffin and being charged for a replacement is the single clearest drag on an otherwise warm sentiment picture — a public owner response acknowledges it without burying it.
